title: Patient Restraints
content_markdown:
#### Indications

- To secure agitated or combative patients to the stretcher.
- The use of this procedure should be strictly limited to: 
- Patients in police custody, with the collaboration of a police officer.
- Patients who are aggressive for organic reasons (e.g. closed head injury).
- Psycho-stimulant abuse or mental health patients. Chemical restraint should be used first if it is safe to do so.

#### Procedure

- Posture patient in supine position.

- Apply wrist restraints securing the Velcro tabs and attach to stretcher rails.

- Apply the ankle restraints securing with Velcro tabs and attach firmly to stretcher rail.

- All restrained patients require monitoring (SaO2, BP, ECG).

- Monitor patient closely, checking distal perfusion of limbs and respiratory effort and need for further sedation.

#### Contraindications

- Do not position the patient prone.

#### Complications

- Vomiting leading to aspiration.
- Hyperthermia and rhabdomyolysis of an inadequately sedated patient.
